info.name: Travel Safari API
info.description:
Complete API collection for Travel Safari platform.

**Test Credentials:**
🔐 Admin:          admin@example.com / admin123
👤 User:           user@example.com / user123
⏰ Expired User:   expired@example.com / expired123
🎁 Gift Expired:   giftexpired@example.com / giftexpired123
✅ Active User:    active@example.com / active123
🔄 Renewed User:   renewed@example.com / renewed123

**Features:**
- User registration with email verification (OTP via Mailchimp)
- Flexible payment amounts (default 999 cents = €9.99)
- Multiple payment methods: Credit/Debit Cards + Apple Pay 🍎
- Gift Subscription: Gift memberships to friends/family 🎁
- Subscription Renewal: Automatic tracking and renewal support 🔄
- Subscription Expiry: Auto-detection and user status updates ⏰
- Password Reset: Secure OTP-based password recovery 🔐
- Contact Form: Public contact form with email notifications 📬
- Automatic Stripe checkout session creation
- JWT token authentication (7-day expiry)
- User management endpoints

**Quick Start:**
1. Register → Get OTP via email
2. Verify OTP (optional: specify amount in CENTS) → Auto-redirects to payment
3. Complete payment (Card or Apple Pay) → Account activated
4. Login → Access granted

OR use existing credentials above for quick testing!

**Subscription Renewal Flow:**
1. Login with expired user (expired@example.com)
2. API will return 403 Forbidden: Subscription expired
3. Create checkout session → Complete payment
4. System detects renewal and marks old subscription as renewed
5. New subscription created → User reactivated
6. Access granted with new subscription

**Gift Subscription Flow:**
1. Login as existing user
2. Create gift checkout session with recipient details
3. Complete payment → Recipient account auto-created
4. Recipient gets email with login credentials
5. Gift expires after 1 year → Reminder sent 1 month before expiry

**Password Reset Flow:**
1. Forgot Password (email) → Get 4-digit OTP via email
2. Verify Reset OTP (email + code) → Reset token returned (valid 5 min)
3. Reset Password (resetToken + newPassword) → Password updated
4. Login with new password → Access granted

**Contact Form Flow:**
1. Submit form (fullName, email, message) → Email sent to admin
2. Admin receives notification with sender details
3. Admin can reply directly to sender's email

**Subscription System Details:**
- Active subscriptions checked on every authenticated request
- Expired subscriptions automatically marked by daily cron job (08:00 AM)
- Users with expired subscriptions set to INACTIVE status
- Renewal detection: Old subscriptions marked as 'isRenewed'
- Multiple subscriptions per user supported (historical tracking)
- Gift subscriptions tracked separately with reminder system

**Field Conventions:**
- Required fields marked in descriptions
- Optional fields have default values
- ⚠️ All monetary amounts sent in CENTS (999 = €9.99)
- Backend converts cents → euros for database storage
- Stripe receives amounts in cents as required
- Password requirements: 8+ chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special char

**Test Details:**
- Base URL: http://localhost:3000
- Card Test: 4242 4242 4242 4242
- Apple Pay: Available on Safari/iOS automatically
- Default Payment: 999 cents (€9.99)

request.description: Check if the API server is running

item.name: Dashboard

request.description:
Get dashboard statistics for admin overview.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Response Data:**
- totalMembers: Number of users with USER role
- activeDeals: Count of active travel deals
- currentGiveaways: Count of active giveaways
- recentSignups: Array of last 5 registered users with:
  - id: User UUID
  - email: User email
  - firstName: First name
  - lastName: Last name
  - fullName: Full name
  - createdAt: Registration date

**Use Case:**
- Admin dashboard overview page
- Display key metrics at a glance
- Show recent user registrations

item.description:
Dashboard statistics for admin overview.

**Endpoint:**
- GET /dashboard - Get dashboard stats

**Features:**
- Total members count (USER role only)
- Active deals count
- Active giveaways count
- Recent sign-ups (last 5 users)

**Security:**
- Admin role required
- Authentication token required

item.name: Authentication Flow

exec: console.log('✅ Registration successful! Check email for OTP.');

request.description:
Register a new user.

**Required Fields (All):**
- firstName: User's first name
- lastName: User's last name
- email: Valid email address
- password: Minimum 6 characters

**Response:**
- User created with status: PENDING
- 4-digit OTP sent to email via Mailchimp
- OTP expires in 10 minutes
- Check email for verification code

exec: console.log('✅ OTP verified! Token saved. User email is now verified.');
console.log('🧪 Test Card: 4242 4242 4242 4242, any future date, any CVC');
console.log('🍎 Apple Pay: Available on Safari/iOS devices automatically');
console.log('👉 Open the URL above in browser to complete payment');

request.description:
Verify the OTP code sent to email. Automatically creates Stripe payment session.

**Required Fields:**
- email: User's email address
- code: 4-digit OTP code from email

**Optional Fields:**
- amount: Payment amount in CENTS (defaults to 999 cents = €9.99)
  - ⚠️ CRITICAL: Send amount in CENTS, not euros!
  - Examples: 999 (€9.99 Basic), 1999 (€19.99 Premium), 2999 (€29.99 Pro)
  - Backend automatically converts cents → euros for database storage
  - Stripe receives the amount in cents as required
  - Always charged in EUR currency

**Special Behavior - Payment Retry:**
If user's email is already verified (isEmailVerified=true) AND status is PENDING:
- Skips OTP verification
- Creates new Stripe payment session immediately
- Allows users who cancelled payment to retry without new OTP
- Returns existing token + new payment URL

**Response:**
- JWT token (store in localStorage, valid 7 days)
- Stripe payment session with sessionId
- Payment URL for redirect to Stripe checkout
- User status changes from PENDING → ready for payment

exec: console.log('✅ New OTP sent! Check email.');
console.log('❌ Failed to resend OTP:', pm.response.json().message);

request.description:
Request a new OTP code if the previous one expired or was not received.

**Required Fields:**
- email: User's email address

**Use Cases:**
- OTP code expired (10 minutes)
- Email not received
- Need to resend verification code

Login with email and password.

**Required Fields (All):**
- email: User's email address
- password: User's password

**Requirements:**
- Email must be verified (isEmailVerified=true)
- User status must be ACTIVE (payment completed)

**Response:**
- JWT token (valid for 7 days)
- User details with active status

Get current authenticated user details.

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token

**No Parameters Required**

**Response:**
- User ID
- Email, firstName, lastName, fullName
- Role (USER/ADMIN)
- Status (ACTIVE/INACTIVE)
- isEmailVerified (true/false)
- homeAirport
- Creation date

**Use Cases:**
- Check if user is logged in
- Display user profile
- Verify account status

Logout current user.

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token

**No Parameters Required**

**What This Does:**
- Invalidates JWT token on server side
- User must login again to access protected routes

**Frontend Action:**
- Remove token from localStorage
- Redirect to login page

**Note:** In stateless JWT setup, logout is primarily handled client-side

exec: console.log('✅ Password reset OTP sent! Check email.');
console.log('❌ Failed to send OTP:', pm.response.json().message);

request.description:
Request a password reset OTP.

**Required Fields:**
- email: User's registered email address

**What This Does:**
1. Checks if user exists with this email
2. Generates a 4-digit OTP code
3. Stores OTP in database (valid for 10 minutes)
4. Sends OTP to user's email via Mailchimp

**OTP Details:**
- 4-digit numeric code
- Expires in 10 minutes
- Can be requested multiple times (old OTPs become invalid)

**Response:**
- Success message confirming OTP was sent
- User should check email for the code

**Use Cases:**
- User forgot their password
- User wants to reset password securely
- Accessed from "Forgot Password" link on login page

**Security:**
- No authentication required (user is not logged in)
- OTP is time-limited
- One-time use only

exec: console.log('🔑 Reset token saved (valid for 5 minutes)');
console.log('👉 You can now proceed to reset your password');

request.description:
Verify the password reset OTP and get a reset token.

**Required Fields:**
- email: User's email address
- code: 4-digit OTP from email

**What This Does:**
1. Validates the OTP code
2. Checks if OTP is expired (10 minutes)
3. Checks if OTP is already used
4. Generates a temporary reset token (valid for 5 minutes)

**Response:**
- resetToken: Short-lived JWT token (5 minutes only)
- Success message

**Validation:**
- OTP must match email
- OTP must not be expired
- OTP must not be already used

**Next Step:**
- Call "Reset Password" endpoint with resetToken + newPassword
- No need to send email/code again!

**Use Cases:**
- Verify user has access to the email account
- Prevent unauthorized password resets
- Part of 3-step password reset flow

**Security:**
- No authentication required
- OTP is single-use
- Reset token expires in 5 minutes
- Token is only valid for password reset

exec: console.log('👉 You can now login with your new password');

request.description:
Reset user password with reset token (from Verify Reset OTP step).

**Required Fields:**
- resetToken: Token from "Verify Reset OTP" step (valid for 5 minutes)
- newPassword: New password meeting requirements

**Password Requirements:**
- Minimum 8 characters
- At least one uppercase letter (A-Z)
- At least one lowercase letter (a-z)
- At least one number (0-9)
- At least one special character (@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{};\':"|,.<>/?)

**What This Does:**
1. Verifies the reset token is valid and not expired
2. Validates new password meets requirements
3. Hashes new password securely
4. Updates user's password in database
5. Marks OTP as used (cannot be reused)

**Response:**
- Success message
- User can now login with new password

**Prerequisites:**
- Must call "Forgot Password" first to get OTP via email
- Must call "Verify Reset OTP" to get reset token (saves automatically in {{reset_token}})
- Token must not be expired (5 minutes validity)

**Complete Password Reset Flow:**
1. Forgot Password (email) → OTP sent to email
2. Verify Reset OTP (email + code) → Reset token returned & saved
3. Reset Password (resetToken + newPassword) → Password updated ← YOU ARE HERE
4. Login with new password → Access granted

**Security:**
- No authentication required for forgotten password flow
- Reset token is short-lived (5 minutes only)
- OTP is marked as used after successful reset
- Password is securely hashed before storage
- Old password is invalidated

**Simplified:**
- No need to send email/code again!
- Just use resetToken (from previous step) + newPassword
- Token expires in 5 minutes after OTP verification

item.description:
Complete authentication flow with flexible payment amounts and password reset.

**Recommended Flow:**
1. Register (firstName, lastName, email, password) - ALL REQUIRED
2. Check email for 4-digit OTP
3. Verify OTP (email, code) + OPTIONAL amount in CENTS - Auto-creates Stripe session
4. Complete payment on Stripe (999 cents = €9.99 default or custom amount)
5. Verify payment (sessionId) - Activates account
6. Login (email, password) - Access granted

**Password Reset Flow:**
1. Forgot Password (email) → OTP sent to email
2. Verify Reset OTP (email, code) → Reset token returned (5 min)
3. Reset Password (resetToken, newPassword) → Password updated
4. Login with new password → Access granted

**Payment Retry Flow:**
If user cancelled Stripe payment after OTP verification:
1. User has token + email verified + status PENDING
2. Option A: Call "Verify OTP" again → Creates new payment session
3. Option B: Call "Complete Payment (Retry)" → Direct to payment without OTP

**Field Requirements:**
- Register: ALL fields required
- Verify OTP: email + code required, amount optional (in CENTS)
- Resend OTP: email required
- Login: email + password required
- Password Reset: 3-step flow with OTP + token verification

**Payment:**
- ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Always send amounts in CENTS
- Default: 999 cents (€9.99)
- Examples: 999 (€9.99), 1999 (€19.99), 2999 (€29.99)
- Backend converts to euros for database storage
- Specified during OTP verification or payment creation

**Password Requirements:**
- Minimum 8 characters
- Uppercase, lowercase, number, special character required

item.name: Payment Flow (Stripe)

request.description:
Get Stripe publishable key for frontend integration.

**No Parameters Required**

**Response:**
- Stripe publishable key (pk_test_...)
- Used for frontend Stripe.js integration

**Note:** This is a public endpoint (no authentication required)

exec: console.log('🔄 User already verified email - skipping OTP');
console.log('Test Card: 4242 4242 4242 4242, any future date, any CVC');
console.log('👉 Open this URL in browser to complete payment');
console.log('❌ Failed to create session:', pm.response.json().message);

request.description:
🔄 **PAYMENT RETRY ENDPOINT** - For users who verified email but didn't complete payment.

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token (from OTP verification)

**User Requirements:**
- Email verified (isEmailVerified = true)
- Status = PENDING (payment not completed)
- Has valid auth token

**When to Use:**
1. User verified OTP → Got payment URL → Cancelled/closed payment
2. User returns later (minutes/hours/days)
3. User clicks "Complete Payment" button
4. Frontend calls THIS endpoint (NOT OTP verification)
5. User redirected DIRECTLY to Stripe

**Amount Format:**
- Send in CENTS (999 = €9.99)
- If omitted, defaults to 999 cents
- Backend passes to Stripe as-is (no multiplication)
- Database stores converted to euros (9.99)

**Advantages Over OTP Flow:**
- ✅ Skips OTP verification (already verified)
- ✅ Faster user experience
- ✅ Works even if OTP expired
- ✅ Creates fresh Stripe session each time
- ✅ Unlimited retry attempts

**Error Responses:**
- 401: Token missing/invalid
- 403: Email not verified (redirect to OTP)
- 400: Already active (redirect to dashboard)

**Response:**
- New Stripe checkout session
- Payment URL for immediate redirect
- Session valid for 24 hours

exec: console.log('Test Card: 4242 4242 4242 4242, any future date, any CVC');
console.log('Apple Pay: Available on Safari/iOS automatically');
console.log('👉 Open this URL in browser to complete payment');
console.log('❌ Failed to create session:', pm.response.json().message);

request.description:
Create Stripe checkout session manually (usually not needed - payment session auto-created during OTP verification).

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token

**Optional Fields:**
- amount: Payment amount in CENTS (defaults to 999 = €9.99)
  - Examples: 999, 1999, 2999

**Requirements:**
- User must be authenticated (JWT token)
- User email must be verified

**Use Cases:**
- Payment session expired (24 hours)
- Retry after failed payment
- User wants to change payment amount

**Response:**
- Stripe checkout session ID
- Payment URL for redirect
- Amount and currency details

exec: console.log('🎉 Account is now ACTIVE! You can login.');

request.description:
Verify payment completion and activate user account.

**Required Fields:**
- sessionId: Stripe checkout session ID

**What This Does:**
1. Checks Stripe payment status
2. Creates subscription record (1 year from payment date)
3. Creates payment record with transaction details
4. Updates user status: PENDING → ACTIVE
5. Sends welcome email (Mailchimp)

**When to Call:**
- After user completes payment on Stripe
- Stripe redirects to: yoursite.com/payment/success?session_id=cs_xxx
- Extract session_id from URL and call this endpoint

**Response:**
- Updated user with ACTIVE status
- Subscription details (start/end dates)
- Payment amount confirmation

exec: console.log('👉 Open this URL in browser to complete gift payment');
console.log('❌ Failed to create gift session:', pm.response.json().message);

request.description:
Create a Stripe checkout session to gift a subscription to another user.

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token (gifter must be logged in)

**Required Fields:**
- recipientEmail: Email address for recipient's account
- recipientPassword: Password for recipient (must meet security requirements)
- recipientFirstName: Recipient's first name
- recipientLastName: Recipient's last name

**Optional Fields:**
- amount: Payment amount in CENTS (defaults to 999 cents = €9.99)
  - ⚠️ CRITICAL: Send amount in CENTS, not euros!
  - Examples: 999 (€9.99), 1999 (€19.99), 2999 (€29.99)
  - Backend automatically converts cents → euros for database storage
  - Stripe receives the amount in cents as required

**Password Requirements:**
- Minimum 8 characters
- At least one uppercase letter (A-Z)
- At least one lowercase letter (a-z)
- At least one number (0-9)
- At least one special character (@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{};\':"|,.<>/?)

**What Happens:**
1. Validates all recipient details
2. Checks recipient email doesn't already exist
3. Creates Stripe checkout session for gifter to pay
4. Stores gift details in session metadata
5. Returns payment URL for gifter

**After Payment:**
- Recipient account is created automatically
- Subscription is activated immediately
- Recipient receives email with login credentials
- No email verification needed for gift recipients

**Use Case:**
- User wants to gift a membership to friend/family
- Accessed from user dashboard "Gift Subscription" option

exec: console.log('🎉 Gift email sent to recipient with login credentials!');
console.log('❌ Gift payment verification failed:', pm.response.json().message);

request.description:
Verify gift payment and create recipient account.

**Required Fields:**
- sessionId: Stripe checkout session ID

**What This Does:**
1. Verifies payment was completed on Stripe
2. Creates new user account for recipient with:
   - Email and password from gift session
   - Email verification bypassed (set to true)
   - Status immediately set to ACTIVE
3. Creates subscription record:
   - 1-year membership
   - Marked as gifted (isGifted: true)
   - Links to gifter (giftedBy: gifter's user ID)
4. Creates payment record
5. Sends gift notification email to recipient with:
   - Who gifted them the subscription
   - Login credentials (email + password)
   - Membership benefits
   - Direct login link

**When to Call:**
- After gifter completes payment on Stripe
- Stripe redirects to: yoursite.com/payment/success?session_id=cs_xxx&gift=true
- Extract session_id from URL and call this endpoint
- Check for gift=true parameter to distinguish from regular payment

**Response:**
- Recipient account details
- Subscription information
- Confirmation that gift email was sent

**Important:**
- Recipient account is created ONLY after successful payment
- Recipient can login immediately (no email verification needed)
- Gifter's name is included in recipient's welcome email

item.description:
Stripe payment integration with flexible amounts (default €9.99).

**Normal Flow (Recommended):**
1. Register → Verify OTP (payment auto-created) → Pay on Stripe → Verify Payment

**Manual Flow (Backup):**
1. Get Config → Create Session → Pay on Stripe → Verify Payment

**Gift Subscription Flow:**
1. Login as User → Create Gift Checkout Session → Pay on Stripe → Verify Gift Payment → Recipient Receives Email

**Payment Details:**
- Currency: EUR (euro)
- Default Amount: €9.99
- Custom amounts supported (frontend can specify)
- Payment Methods: Credit/Debit Cards + Apple Pay 🍎
- Test Card: 4242 4242 4242 4242
- Apple Pay: Auto-available on Safari/iOS

item.name: User Management

request.description:
Get current authenticated user's profile details.

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token

**Response:**
- User ID, email, firstName, lastName, fullName
- Role, status, isEmailVerified
- homeAirport
- createdAt, updatedAt

Update current user's profile information.

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token

**Required Fields:**
- currentPassword: Current password for verification
- newPassword: New password (min 8 characters)

**Optional Fields:**
- firstName: Update first name
- lastName: Update last name

**Restrictions:**
- Email cannot be changed
- Current password verification required
- New password must be at least 8 characters

**Response:**
- Updated user profile (password not included)

Update user's home airport.

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token

**Required Field:**
- homeAirport: Airport code (e.g., LAX, JFK, LHR)

**No Password Required**

**Response:**
- Updated user profile with new home airport

Get all users with pagination, search, filtering, and sorting.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Query Parameters (All Optional):**
- page: Page number (default: 1)
- limit: Results per page (default: 10)
- search: Search by name or email
- status: Filter by status (ACTIVE or INACTIVE)
- sortBy: Sort field (createdAt, email, etc.)
- sortOrder: asc or desc

**Response:**
- users: Array of user objects
- pagination: Page info (total, pages, hasNext, hasPrev)

Get specific user by ID.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Path Parameter:**
- id: User UUID

**Response:**
- Full user profile details

Update user details (Admin only).

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Path Parameter:**
- id: User UUID

**Optional Fields:**
- firstName: Update first name
- lastName: Update last name
- homeAirport: Update home airport
- status: ACTIVE or INACTIVE
- role: USER/ADMIN

**Restrictions:**
- Email cannot be changed
- Password cannot be changed (user must update themselves)
- No password verification required for admin updates

**Response:**
- Updated user profile

Delete user by ID.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Path Parameter:**
- id: User UUID

**Warning:**
- Permanent deletion
- Cannot be undone

item.description:
User profile management and admin operations.

**User Endpoints:**
- GET /users/me - Get current user profile
- PUT /users/me - Update profile (requires current & new password)
- PUT /users/me/home-airport - Update home airport (no password)

**Admin Endpoints:**
- GET /users - List all users with pagination
- GET /users/:id - Get specific user
- PUT /users/:id - Update any user (no password required)
- DELETE /users/:id - Delete any user

**Security:**
- Profile updates require password verification
- Email cannot be changed by anyone
- Users cannot delete their own account
- Admin endpoints require ADMIN role
- Admin can update user status and role

item.name: Travel Guides Management

request.description: Get top 3 travel guides (Public - no authentication required). Returns latest 3 guides.
Get all travel guides with pagination and search (Authenticated users).
Get specific travel guide by ID (Authenticated users).

Get all travel guides with pagination and search.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Query Parameters (All Optional):**
- page: Page number (default: 1)
- limit: Results per page (default: 10)
- search: Search by title or location
- sortBy: Sort field (createdAt, title, etc.)
- sortOrder: asc or desc

**Response:**
- guides: Array of travel guide objects
- pagination: Page info (total, pages, hasNext, hasPrev)

Get specific travel guide by ID.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Path Parameter:**

**Response:**
- Complete guide details including content structure

request.description:
Create new travel guide with image upload.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Form Data Fields:**
- heroImage: Image file (optional) - Backend will convert to URL
- title: Guide title (required)
- description: Brief description of the guide (required)
- category: Guide category - Cultural, Adventure, Food, Nature, etc. (required)
- location: City/country (required)
- readTime: Reading time (e.g., 10 min) (required)
- content: JSON string of content array (required)

**Content Structure (as JSON string):**
Each tab object contains:
- tabName: Tab label (e.g., Airport Transport)
- mainTitle: Section title
- sections: Array of items with:
  - subHeading: Item title
  - description: Item content

**How it works:**
1. Frontend sends image file + JSON data
2. Backend uploads image and generates URL
3. Saves guide with auto-generated image URL
4. Returns complete guide with accessible image link

**Response:**
- Created travel guide with ID and heroImage URL

formdata.value: Updated description: Discover the stunning capital of the UAE with its blend of tradition and modernity.

request.description:
Update existing travel guide with optional image upload.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Path Parameter:**
- id: Travel Guide UUID

**Form Data Fields (all optional):**
- heroImage: New image file - Backend will convert to URL
- title: Guide title
- description: Brief description of the guide
- category: Guide category - Cultural, Adventure, Food, Nature, etc.
- location: City/country
- readTime: Reading time
- content: Complete content array as JSON string

**How it works:**
1. If new image uploaded, backend generates new URL
2. Updates only the fields you send
3. Returns updated guide with image URL

**Note:**
- Only send fields you want to update
- If updating content, send the complete content array
- Partial content updates not supported

**Response:**
- Updated travel guide with image URL

Delete travel guide by ID.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Path Parameter:**
- id: Travel Guide UUID

**Warning:**
- Permanent deletion
- Cannot be undone

item.description:
Travel guides management for admin.

**All Endpoints:**
- GET /travel-guides - List all guides with pagination/search
- GET /travel-guides/:id - Get specific guide
- POST /travel-guides - Create new guide
- PUT /travel-guides/:id - Update guide
- DELETE /travel-guides/:id - Delete guide

**Content Structure:**
Guides have dynamic content organized in tabs:
- Each tab contains a main title and sections
- Each section has a sub-heading and description
- Supports markdown formatting in descriptions

**Security:**
- All endpoints require ADMIN role
- Authentication token required

**Response:**

Get featured travel deals (Public - no authentication required). Use query params for pagination.

query.description: Search in title, destination, or airport
Sort by field (createdAt, price, title, etc.)

request.description: Get all travel deals with pagination and search (Authenticated users).
Get specific travel deal by ID (Authenticated users).

Get single travel deal by ID.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Path Parameter:**
- id: Travel Deal UUID

**Response:**
- Complete deal information

request.description:
Create new travel deal with image upload.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Form Data Fields:**
- dealImage: Image file (optional) - Backend will convert to URL
- title: Deal title (required)
- description: Deal description (required)
- price: Deal price (required)
- discount: Discount percentage 0-100 (optional)
- destination: Destination city/country (required)
- airport: Airport category (required)
- travelStartDate: Start date YYYY-MM-DD (optional)
- travelEndDate: End date YYYY-MM-DD (optional)
- flightBookingLink: Flight booking URL (optional)
- hotelBookingLink: Hotel booking URL (optional)
- status: ACTIVE or EXPIRED (optional, default: ACTIVE)

**How it works:**
1. Frontend sends image file + form data
2. Backend uploads image and generates URL
3. Saves deal with auto-generated image URL
4. Returns complete deal with accessible image link

**Note:** Use Toggle Featured Status endpoint to mark deals as featured.

**Response:**
- Created travel deal with ID and dealImage URL

item.description:
Travel deals management for admin.

**All Endpoints:**
- GET /travel-deals - List all deals with pagination/filtering
- GET /travel-deals/:id - Get specific deal
- POST /travel-deals - Create new deal with image upload
- PUT /travel-deals/:id - Update deal with optional image upload
- DELETE /travel-deals/:id - Delete deal
- PATCH /travel-deals/:id/status - Update deal status
- PATCH /travel-deals/:id/featured - Toggle featured status

**Features:**
- Image upload support (dealImage field)
- Automatic image URL generation
- Search by title, destination, or airport
- Filter by status (ACTIVE/EXPIRED) and featured
- Pagination support
- Discount percentage
- Travel date range
- Booking links (flight and hotel)

**Security:**
- All endpoints require ADMIN role
- Authentication token required

item.name: Giveaway Management

request.description:
Get currently active giveaway for homepage display.

**Authentication:** Not required

**Access:** Public (both logged-in and logged-out users)

**Features:**
- No authentication token needed
- Returns the first active giveaway found
- Includes giveaway image, title, description, dates
- Includes entry count
- Used for homepage display to all visitors

**Response:**
- id: Giveaway UUID
- title: Giveaway title
- description: Giveaway description
- giveawayImage: Image URL
- startDate: Start date/time
- endDate: End date/time
- status: Current status (ACTIVE, UPCOMING, EXPIRED, DRAFT)
- _count.entries: Number of entries/participants

**Use Case:**
- Homepage giveaway section
- Display same giveaway to all users (login/logout)
- Contest preview before login
Get active giveaways for authenticated users to view and enter.
Get specific giveaway details for authenticated user.

query.description: Filter by status: DRAFT, UPCOMING, ACTIVE, or EXPIRED
Sort by field (createdAt, title, startDate, etc.)

request.description:
Get all giveaways with pagination, filtering, and search.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Query Parameters:**
- page: Page number (default: 1)
- limit: Items per page (default: 10, max: 50)
- search: Search in title or description
- status: Filter by DRAFT, UPCOMING, ACTIVE, or EXPIRED
- sortBy: Field to sort by (createdAt, title, startDate, etc.)
- sortOrder: Sort direction (asc/desc)

**Status Auto-Update:**
- UPCOMING: startDate > now
- ACTIVE: startDate <= now && endDate >= now
- EXPIRED: endDate < now
- DRAFT: Manual status (not auto-updated)

**Response:**
- Array of giveaways with entry count and pagination metadata

Get single giveaway by ID with entry count.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Path Parameter:**
- id: Giveaway UUID

**Response:**
- Giveaway details with entry count

request.description:
Create new giveaway with image upload.

**Authentication Required:** Admin role

**Form Data Fields:**
- giveawayImage: Image file (optional) - Backend will convert to URL
- title: Giveaway title (required)
- description: Giveaway description (required)
- startDate: Start date YYYY-MM-DD (required)
- endDate: End date YYYY-MM-DD (required)
- isMonthlyActive: true or false (optional, default: false)

**Auto Status:**
- UPCOMING if startDate > now
- ACTIVE if between start and end
- EXPIRED if endDate < now

**Response:**
- Created giveaway with ID and image URL

Update existing giveaway with optional image upload.

Get all entries for a specific giveaway.

Check user's entry status for the active giveaway. Returns whether user has already entered.

**Authentication Required:** Any authenticated user

**No Parameters Required**

**Response:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "giveaway": {
      "id": "giveaway-id",
      "title": "Win a trip in paris",
      "description": "...",
      "giveawayImage": "...",
      "status": "ACTIVE",
      "_count": { "entries": 150 }
    },
    "hasEntered": true,           // ← MAIN FIELD: true if already entered, false if not
    "entryCount": 150,             // Total number of entries
    "userEnteredAt": "2026-03-04T04:14:44.318Z"  // When user entered (null if not entered)
  }
}
```

**Frontend Usage:**
1. Call this endpoint on page load
2. Check `hasEntered` field
3. If true: Disable "Enter Now" button and show "Already Entered"
4. If false: Enable button
5. After user clicks enter → call "Enter Giveaway" endpoint
6. Response will have `hasEntered: true`

**Use Cases:**
- Check entry status on page load
- Update button state based on user's participation
- Show when user entered the giveaway
- Display total participant count

**Key Features:**
- ✅ Works on page reload (backend checks, not localStorage)
- ✅ Real-time status update
- ✅ Shows user's entry timestamp
- ✅ Shows total entry count
- ✅ No need for multiple endpoints

User enters/participates in a giveaway.

**Authentication Required:** Any authenticated user (not admin-only)

**Path Parameter:**
- id: Giveaway UUID

**No body required** - User info is extracted from token:
- User ID from token
- Email from token
- Name from token
- Entry timestamp auto-generated

**Validation:**
- Giveaway must exist
- Giveaway must be ACTIVE (not DRAFT, UPCOMING, or EXPIRED)
- User can only enter once per giveaway

**Response (UPDATED - Now includes hasEntered):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Successfully entered the giveaway",
  "data": {
    "entry": {
      "id": "entry-id",
      "giveawayId": "giveaway-id",
      "userId": "user-id",
      "enteredAt": "2026-03-04T04:14:44.318Z",
      "giveaway": {
        "id": "giveaway-id",
        "title": "Win a trip in paris",
        "status": "ACTIVE"
      }
    },
    "hasEntered": true  // ← NEW FIELD: Always true on successful entry
  }
}
```

**Frontend Implementation:**
```javascript
const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/giveaways/${giveawayId}/enter`, {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}` }
});
const data = await response.json();

if (data.success) {
  setHasEntered(data.data.hasEntered);  // Set to true
  setButtonDisabled(true);                // Disable button
  setButtonText('Already Entered');       // Update text
}
```

**Error Cases:**
- 404: Giveaway not found
- 400: Giveaway not active (must be ACTIVE status)
- 400: Already entered this giveaway (user tried to enter twice)

item.description:
Giveaway management for admin and user participation.

**All Endpoints:**
- GET /giveaways/public/active - Get active giveaway (Public - no auth)
- GET /giveaways/active - Get active giveaway (User)
- GET /giveaways/:id - Get specific giveaway
- GET /giveaways - List all giveaways (Admin)
- GET /giveaways/:id/entries - Get all entries (Admin)
- POST /giveaways - Create new giveaway (Admin)
- PUT /giveaways/:id - Update giveaway (Admin)
- DELETE /giveaways/:id - Delete giveaway (Admin)
- **GET /giveaways/check-status - Check user's entry status (NEW ⭐)**
- **POST /giveaways/:id/enter - User enters giveaway**

**NEW FEATURE: Check Entry Status**
- Endpoint: `GET /api/v1/giveaways/check-status`
- Returns: `hasEntered` (true/false), `entryCount`, `userEnteredAt`
- Perfect for disabling "Enter Now" button if user already entered
- Works on page reload (backend checks, not localStorage)

**Features:**
- Image upload support (giveawayImage field)
- Automatic status updates based on dates
- Search by title or description
- Filter by status (DRAFT, UPCOMING, ACTIVE, EXPIRED)
- Pagination support
- Monthly active flag
- Entry tracking with user details
- User participation (one entry per user)
- Entry status checking

**Auto Status Logic:**
- UPCOMING: startDate > now
- ACTIVE: Between start and end date
- EXPIRED: endDate < now
- DRAFT: Manual status (not auto-updated)

**Security:**
- Admin endpoints require ADMIN role
- User endpoints require authentication
- Entry endpoints automatically validate user access

item.name: Image Upload

request.description:
Upload single image file.

**Authentication Required:** Yes

**Form Data:**
- image: Image file (JPEG, JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP)

**File Limits:**
- Max size: 5MB
- Allowed formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP

**Response:**
Returns image URL and metadata

Upload multiple image files at once.

**Authentication Required:** Yes

**Form Data:**
- images: Multiple image files (max 10)

**File Limits:**
- Max files: 10
- Max size per file: 5MB
- Allowed formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP

**Response:**
Returns array of image URLs and metadata

Delete uploaded image.

**Authentication Required:** Yes

**Path Parameter:**
- filename: Name of the file to delete

**Note:**
- Only deletes the physical file
- Permanent deletion

item.description:
Image upload management using Multer.

**All Endpoints:**
- POST /upload/image - Upload single image
- POST /upload/images - Upload multiple images (max 10)
- DELETE /upload/image/:filename - Delete image

**File Handling:**
- Images saved locally in /uploads directory
- Works on any server (local or deployed)
- Unique filenames generated automatically
- Returns accessible URLs for each uploaded image

**Supported Formats:**
- JPEG / JPG
- PNG
- GIF
- WebP

**Limits:**
- Max file size: 5MB per image
- Max files in multiple upload: 10

**Security:**
- All endpoints require authentication
- File type validation enforced
- Size limits enforced

item.name: Contact Form

exec: console.log('❌ Contact form submission failed:', pm.response.json().message);

request.description:
Submit a contact form message to the admin team.

**Public Endpoint:** No authentication required

**Required Fields:**
- fullName: Sender's full name
- email: Valid email address (format validated)
- message: Message content (10-1000 characters)

**What This Does:**
1. Validates all input fields
2. Checks email format
3. Validates message length (10-1000 chars)
4. Sends email notification to admin (info@travelinaclick.ie)
5. Email includes sender's details and message
6. Reply-to automatically set to sender's email

**Response:**
- Success message confirming submission
- User receives confirmation

**Email Features:**
- Beautiful HTML template
- Sender's name and email
- Message with proper formatting
- Submission timestamp
- Quick reply button
- Admin can reply directly by responding to email

**Validation Rules:**
- Full name: Required, non-empty
- Email: Required, valid format
- Message: Required, 10-1000 characters

**Use Cases:**
- Contact us page on website
- Customer inquiries
- Support requests
- General questions

**Security:**
- Server-side validation
- Spam protection via message length limits
- Email format validation
- Rate limiting (can be added if needed)

**Error Responses:**
- 400: Missing fields or invalid data
- 500: Email service failure

item.description:
Contact form submission endpoint.

**Endpoint:**
- POST /contact/submit - Submit contact form

**Public Access:**
- No authentication required
- Anyone can submit contact forms

**Features:**
- Email notification to admin (info@travelinaclick.ie)
- Beautiful HTML email template
- Automatic reply-to sender's email
- Input validation (email format, message length)
- Professional confirmation message

**Validation:**
- Full name: Required
- Email: Required, valid format
- Message: 10-1000 characters

**Use Case:**
- Website contact form
- Customer support inquiries
- General questions
- Business inquiries

**Email Template Includes:**
- Sender's full name
- Sender's email (clickable)
- Message content (formatted)
- Submission date/time
- Reply button
- Professional branding
